title: cymbopogon diplandrus
intro: Cymbopogon diplandrus, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family, characterized by its long, slender stems and fragrant leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its essential oil content. The health benefits of East Indian lemongrass include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and pain management, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. East Indian lemongrass can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
